confirmed: ROUND1: on GLD and QQQ (both vol and dollar axes), EVERY labeler (carry, kmeans2stage, hmm) collapses to EXACTLY the always_long buy-and-hold result (1 trade, identical Calmar/DA). Replicated 2 tickers x 2 axes.
ROUND1: n_cells=5-9 per train run => featured labelers DID fit and save non-degenerate cells. The collapse is NOT a labeling failure.

ROUND1 ROOT CAUSE: the bottleneck is MODULE 8 (sizing/threshold), NOT axis or labeling. Infer rule w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) saturates to full-long when p>tau+0.005; isotonic-calibrated probs sit far above the low fixed tau (0.35 GLD / 0.15 QQQ) for the whole OOS window => pinned 100% long => 1 trade == buy-and-hold for every cell.
ROUND1: hmm baseline == always_long on both assets (no separation).

ROUND1 CONFIRM: a NEW featured labeler (trend_leg = forward-drawdown leg labels, K=1.0 sigma band, horizon selected on TRAIN balance) was added and run on GLD vol + QQQ vol. It ALSO tied buy-and-hold EXACTLY (GLD 1.6165/24.0211/1-trade; QQQ 1.1099/22.8298/1-trade == always_long). This is independent confirmation that the bottleneck is module 8 sizing, NOT the labeler: a better label cannot express itself while w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) pins 100% long. The labeler was reverted (no OOS lift over baseline).

ROUND2: DE-SATURATION CONFIRMED. Replaced w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) with de Prado CDF bet w=clip(2*Phi((p-.5)/sqrt(p(1-p)))-1,0,1) * inverse-vol overlay clip(std_100/std_20,0.25,1.0), applied IDENTICALLY in trainer.realistic_cstats (VAL synth) and templates/infer.py.tmpl (OOS), causal (vol from past bar returns only). QQQ vol: trades 1->431, DA 22.83->7.07 (-69%), but REAL Calmar 1.11->0.54 (HALVED). The bottleneck is broken (trades>>1, DA crushed) but the overlay OVER-DE-LEVERS on a drift asset: MaxDD shrank, CAGR shrank MORE, so Calmar fell below buy-hold. Reverted (Calmar regressed).

ROUND2: carry == always_long to 4 dp under the new sizing (both 0.5387 / 7.0697 / 431 trades). The outcome is driven ENTIRELY by the vol-targeting overlay, INDEPENDENT of the labeler (a real AUC-0.80 model and a constant-long signal give the identical exposure path). Re-confirms R1: the labeler is not the lever; module 8 is.

ROUND2: the new VAL synth_cal = -0.3511 CORRECTLY predicted OOS underperformance (R1 synth_cal was a flat uninformative 0.0). Restoring train/infer sizing consistency restored a usable in-sample selection signal.

ROUND3: a binary stay-long-by-default DD circuit-breaker (flat at 12% underwater, re-enter within 4% of peak, hysteresis) on QQQ vol made BOTH metrics WORSE: Calmar 1.11->0.26 AND DA 22.83->76.54 (3x worse). On a drift-up asset with V-shaped recoveries, stepping flat sells the dip and the hysteresis re-enters only near new highs => the strategy is flat through the entire rebound => equity stays underwater far longer (DA explodes) and CAGR collapses. Drawdown STOPS are PRO-CYCLICAL on mean-reverting-up assets. carry==always_long once more (breaker dominates). Reverted.

ROUND4: de-tuned active sizing (CDF recentered on tau x inverse-vol, floor 0.6) on VOL axis, 7 ETFs. Recovered QQQ Calmar 0.54->0.90 (629 trades, DA 14.9<buy-hold) - best active config - but NO ETF beats its buy-hold Calmar; XLE collapses 0.11, EEM under-trades (23, G2 fail). carry==always_long AGAIN. Conclusion: 3 rounds of SIZING tweaks on the vol axis never beat buy-hold => the bottleneck was likely ① axis x ② labeler being WRONG for the asset, not ⑧ sizing.

ROUND5: dollar axis x {kmeans2stage,carry,always_long} x 7 ETFs (2-node parallel). EVERY labeler IDENTICAL to 4dp within each ETF (even IWM where the model has AUC 0.88). History hypothesis 'kmeans2stage beats carry' FALSE/untestable. ROOT CAUSE (key finding): the inverse-vol overlay g=clip(sigma_slow/sigma_fast,0.6,1) is LABEL-INDEPENDENT and dominates the position path; on up-trending ETFs calibrated p is mostly high so _cdf_bet(p)~1 for every labeler => w~g, identical regardless of label. 'Labeler irrelevant' (R1-R5) is an ARTIFACT OF THE SIZING OVERLAY, not the axis/labels. Best ACTIVE configs so far: EEM dollar 1.33 (DA 4.4, 113 trades, G2 pass), HYG dollar 1.26 (DA 4.0, 1310 trades). Dollar genuinely helps EEM/HYG vs vol. Still << G1 (3.0). TLT -0.15 (neg control).

ROUND6: turned the inverse-vol overlay OFF (VOL_FLOOR=1.0, w=cdf_bet(p,tau)). Result: EVERY cell = 1 trade = buy-and-hold, all labelers IDENTICAL. With no overlay the position never goes flat because on up-trending ETFs the calibrated p is almost always > tau (model predicts 'up' nearly everywhere) -> always long -> buy-hold. So overlay-ON (R5) washes the label via the overlay; overlay-OFF (R6) washes it via the always-long model. SIX-ROUND CONCLUSION (confirmed, not assumed): single-asset long-only ML on trending ETFs CANNOT beat buy-hold; axis/labeler/sizing are all washed out. Best single ~1.6 (HYG 1.65, GLD 1.62 buy-hold). The >3.0 frontier is CROSS-ASSET (archive pair 2.91 >> single 1.6).

ROUND7 (first valid v2 tournament; 3 harness bugs fixed first - cell-key collision, dot-in-ObjectStore-path, infer fallback-guard clobbered by render). TLT, binary sizing both legs: A vol/carry/0.55 = -0.1431/17 trades (G2 FAIL, under-trades); B dollar/kmeans2stage/0.45 = 0 trades (never crossed thresh). DISCARD. Learning: binary@high-thresh under-trades TLT; and TLT long-only over a rate-rise can only be positive by being FLAT in declines, but flat-most-of-time can't reach 80 trades -> TLT likely unwinnable long-only (wants a short/relative leg = pairs).

ROUND8: TLT cdf_plain (partial long-only). vol/carry=-0.2852/56tr (WORSE: partial-long still loses on a declining asset); dollar/kmeans2stage=0 trades. DISCARD. CONFIRMS long-only cannot beat a declining bond. UNLOCK (user): shorting now allowed -> longshort/ls_cdf sizing go negative. R9 shorts TLT's rate-rise declines.

ROUND9: TLT long-short (shorting unlocked). ls_cdf=-0.2929/60tr (WORSE than long-only), longshort=0tr. Shorting did NOT help -> the bottleneck is the SIGNAL (carry/kmeans don't predict TLT direction), not the sizing. TLT EXHAUSTED after 3 tournaments. Loop moves to next-weakest = IWM (0.65).

ROUND10: IWM. B dollar/carry/cdf_plain=+0.2532/564tr DEPLOYABLE (first clean active+G2 config of the tournament) but < best 0.65 -> discard. A vol/kmeans2stage/cdf_overlay=0 trades. PATTERN: kmeans2stage repeatedly yields 0 trades in single-config mode (TLT R8/R9, IWM R10) -> its calibrated p sits in too narrow a band to cross tau; prefer carry/bgm/tertile for active configs.

ROUND11: IWM vol/bgm/cdf_overlay = Calmar 1.131, DA 5.6 but 24 trades (G2 FAIL). bgm gives high-edge low-DA signal but UNDER-TRADES -> activity (G2) is the binding constraint, not edge. Lead for IWM later: bgm at lower thresh / more-active sizing. (Per policy, stay on weakest=TLT first.)

ROUND12: TLT directional+short BREAKTHROUGH direction. tertile/logdollar/ls_cdf = -0.0521 (best TLT yet, vs -0.15) but 71 trades (G2 fail by 9). triple_barrier/dollar/ls_cdf = -0.2068/284tr (deployable but worse). LESSON: directional label (tertile) + ls_cdf short/long lifts TLT from -0.15 to ~break-even; the gap is ACTIVITY -> same recipe on a denser axis (dollar/tick) for >80 trades. R13 pushes it.

ROUND13 WIN: TLT -0.1543 -> +0.0413 (KEPT, deployable 151tr). Recipe: directional tertile + ls_cdf (short decline/long recovery) + DENSE dollar axis. B tick/tertile/ls_cdf = +0.4077 (DA 16!) but 59 trades -> tick-class sampling has HIGHER edge, just under-trades. Path to beat IWM(0.65): get tick-class edge to >80 trades.

ROUND15 WIN: TLT +0.0413 -> +0.3122 (KEPT, deployable 234tr, DA 11). range/triple_barrier/ls_cdf. The RANGE axis is the unlock: dense (clears G2) AND concentrated at the rate-shock moves where the edge is -> directional triple_barrier + short ls_cdf gets both edge and activity (what bar-sparse tick couldn't). TLT -0.15->+0.04->+0.31 in 3 kept steps. Still < IWM 0.65; keep pushing range axis.

ROUND16: range/tertile/ls_cdf +0.20/51tr, range/triple_barrier/longshort +0.18/49tr -> both G2 fail. The R15 winner recipe is specific: triple_barrier+ls_cdf is the ACTIVE one on range (234tr); tertile (drops mid-33%) and binary longshort under-trade (~50). TLT best stays +0.3122. Sizing=ls_cdf (continuous) + a label that signals on most bars (triple_barrier) is what clears G2 on range.

ROUND17: range/bgm/ls_cdf +0.02/212tr (deployable but low), range/agglomerative -0.03/38tr. On range, triple_barrier is UNIQUELY the high-edge labeler (+0.31 vs bgm +0.02 / agglom -0.03 / tertile +0.20@51tr). TLT deployable best PLATEAUS at +0.3122 (R15). Higher edge only on bar-capped tick (tick/tertile +0.41@59tr, un-deployable).

ROUND19: IWM (new weakest). TLT recipe logdollar/triple_barrier/ls_cdf ports to +0.50/308tr (deployable, DA 4.2) but < IWM best 0.65; range/triple_barrier/ls_cdf = -0.08 (SHORT legs HURT trending-up IWM). LESSON: IWM != TLT - IWM trends UP so long-biased sizing wins; the lead is bgm (R11 1.13 edge, 24tr). Make bgm deployable on a DENSE axis.

ROUND20 WIN: IWM 0.6542 -> +1.1046 (KEPT, deployable 394tr). logdollar/bgm/cdf_overlay. GENERALIZABLE: bgm has real edge but needs a DENSE axis for G2; cdf_overlay (long-biased) suits trending equities, ls_cdf (short) suits two-sided/declining (TLT). 2 ETFs solved (TLT 0.75, IWM 1.10). New weakest = XLE (0.72).

XLE is TRENDING-UP not two-sided: the TLT short recipe (range/triple_barrier/ls_cdf) went NEGATIVE -0.06 on XLE. bgm (logdollar/cdf_overlay) cut DA 39.27->9.09 (real drawdown-timing) but cdf_overlay's vol-throttle sacrificed too much upside (0.72->0.60). R6 null resurfacing for a strongly-trending asset. XLE needs only +0.03 to overtake TLT(0.75).

dead_ends: ROUND1: sweeping axis (vol vs dollar) and labeler (carry/kmeans2stage/hmm) with the CURRENT sizing is futile -- all 16 cells tie buy-and-hold. Do NOT keep iterating on axis/label choice until module 8 sizing de-saturates.

ROUND1: adding the trend_leg labeler alone (no sizing change) does NOT de-saturate -- it ties buy-and-hold like every other labeler. Reverted. A new labeler is only worth testing AFTER module 8 sizing genuinely modulates exposure on p; bring trend_leg BACK in round 2+ paired with the de-saturated sizing rule.

ROUND2: a CDF map centered at p=0.5 + an aggressive inverse-vol overlay (floor 0.25, fast=20/slow=100) is TOO AGGRESSIVE for drift assets -- it cuts CAGR faster than MaxDD and halves Calmar. Do NOT use floor 0.25 / center-0.5 again. The DA win (-69%) is real but useless if CAGR collapses. The fix is to STAY INVESTED BY DEFAULT and only de-lever in genuine turbulence.

ROUND3: binary realized-drawdown STOP overlays (step flat past an underwater threshold, re-enter near peak) are a DEAD END on drift-up / V-shaped-recovery assets (QQQ): they sell the dip, miss the rebound, and WORSEN both Calmar (1.11->0.26) and DA (22.83->76.54), and fail G2 (2 trades). Do NOT retry drawdown-stop / de-risking overlays on drift assets in ANY form (continuous R2, binary R3 both failed). Such a stop may only help an asset with SUSTAINED (non-V) bear legs.

ROUND4: forcing the VOL axis on all 7 ETFs is wrong - it is the wrong axis for equities (QQQ/IWM); and pairing it with carry (which fails on equities) makes every cell tie/lose to buy-hold. Use per-ETF optimal axis + kmeans2stage (history hypothesis, under test R5). Stop tweaking sizing on the vol axis.

ROUND5: a label-INDEPENDENT inverse-vol overlay multiplied onto the bet makes the labeler untestable (it dominates the path). To test whether ② labeling matters, the sizing must be LABEL-DRIVEN (position responds to the model prob / goes flat when p<=tau), not a vol overlay.

ROUND6: removing the overlay does NOT make the label matter -- it just reverts to buy-hold (model is always-long on trending assets). The single-asset axis x labeling x sizing space is EXHAUSTED (null): do not run more single-asset long-only rounds expecting to beat buy-hold. Pivot to cross-asset pairs.
gates: HARD, on REAL OOS: G1 Calmar>3.0, G2 trades>80, G3 no-lookahead, G4 |train_auc-val_auc|<0.05. G2 EXEMPTS the buy-and-hold baseline (1 trade) -- it is the reference CEILING, not a deployable result. Every DEPLOYABLE config must trade >80 times. So buy-hold (1 trade) and the R3 breaker (2 trades) cannot be 'kept' regardless of Calmar; only an ACTIVELY-trading config (R2 gave 431) can pass.
next_idea: OIL-REVERSION CONSOLIDATED + QUANTIFIED: add USO(1x) to the book -> Calmar 4.617->5.160 (+12%), Sharpe 2.460->2.756 (+12%), MaxDD 2.46->2.82%. CLEAN upgrade. Vehicle = USO not UCO (UCO 2x injects vol: +4% Calmar but -12% Sharpe / 2x MaxDD). PROPOSED book(7) = GLD/UUP/IWM/TIP/DBC/HYG + USO. This is the deployable recommendation -> surface to user/Opus for the crown (commit + re-deploy). The oil-reversion arc (R1196-1206) is COMPLETE: discovered->permute->decay->cost->DSR->book-additive(USO). FRONTIER: single-ticker converging; next big levers user-gated (spread path / new data). Remaining low-EV single-ticker: marginal name re-validation. AVOID: sliced_wasserstein, leveraged-equity/non-oil-commodity reversion, cash-flat. Uncommitted: labeler.py (+sticky_hmm), run_autoresearch_round.py (+allowlist), portfolio_rederive.py (+USO/UCO), beta/mechanism/decay/cost_oil scripts -> human/Opus commits.
frontier: CROSS-ASSET PAIRS. Single-asset long-only ML is confirmed-exhausted at the buy-hold ceiling (~1.6) over 6 rounds. >3.0 requires pairs/spread (history: 2.91), which needs a 2-symbol harness extension (sign-off-worthy scorer change).
notes: clean slate 2026-06-01. R1=landscape, bottleneck=module 8 sizing. R2=de-saturated CDF-x-vol sizing: cleared G2 (431 trades)+DA -69% on QQQ vol but over-de-levered => Calmar 1.11->0.54, reverted. R3=binary DD circuit-breaker: FAILED G2 (2 trades)+worse on both (Calmar 0.26, DA 76.5), reverted -- drawdown stops are pro-cyclical on drift-up assets. REFRAME (user): G2>80 trades EXEMPTS buy-hold as reference; every deployable config MUST trade actively. R2's active sizing is the right regime; tune it (raise floor, recenter CDF on tau) to keep CAGR. carry==always_long in both R2 and R3 => labeler is not the lever; sizing is. | R4: vol-for-all+detuned-sizing => active but sub-buy-hold on all; carry==always_long (4th). PIVOT (history-informed, user-confirmed re-validation): per-ETF optimal axis + kmeans2stage. Audit fix applied: VAL inverse-vol off-by-one (mild VAL-only lookahead) -> append log_rets[i-1] for exact train/infer parity; cost dead-band unified to 0.01. REAL OOS numbers unaffected (from infer). R5 uses the 2-node PARALLEL driver.

FINDING (independence test PASS): oil-reversion is INSTRUMENT-TRANSCENDING. XOP (oil E&P EQUITY) logdollar/revert reproduces 1.0782 (433 tr, val_auc 0.996); permute collapses 77% -> 0.2493 (below BH 0.299, val_auc->0.46). real edge +0.78, permute PASS. => oil-PRICE reversion is real across futures (USO/UCO) AND equity (XOP), NOT a futures-roll/contango artifact. BUT XOP is a POOR vehicle: skew -0.88 / kurt 18.1 (energy-equity crash tails vs UCO's symmetric +0.02), and XOP's own REGIME recipe (imbalance/bgm+ker 1.626) BEATS revert (1.078) — equity beta adds regime structure + dilutes reversion. Family fully characterized: reversion cleanest/strongest on pure oil-futures (UCO best). ONE reversion seat = UCO/USO, not XOP.

FINDING (decay gate PASS): oil-reversion is decay-HEALTHY (strengthening, NOT front-loaded). UCO early-half OOS Sharpe 0.89 -> late 2.57 (2.9x); USO 1.38 -> 2.08 (1.5x); flag_decay=HOLDING both (decay_monitor via scripts/decay_oil.py, real QC equity curve, 223 pts/710d). Remaining gate: cost-stress (UCO 2x turnover @5/10bp) + DSR.

FINDING (cost gate PASS; vehicle decided): UCO is the oil-reversion book vehicle. Cost-stress (scripts/cost_oil.py, ConstantSlippageModel replay): UCO 3.51->3.01@5bp->2.56@10bp (erosion 27%, 552 ord); USO 2.18->1.86->1.55 (29%, 601 ord). UCO @10bp 2.56 ~ GLD-tier (GLD nets ~2.8@10bp), oil-decorrelated, symmetric skew +0.02 (timing sidesteps the 2x leveraged crash tail). FINDING (DSR gate — oil-reversion CLEARS it): session-scale honest_audit DSR: USO 0.939, UCO 0.915 — TIED with GLD 0.941 (book flagship) and FAR above SEATED UUP 0.461 / IWM 0.671. Audit also reconfirms artifacts SSO 0.05 / SPXL 0.02 / IAU 0.72. NUANCE: on DSR, USO(0.939,1x) edges UCO(0.915,2x) [higher per-obs Sharpe + lower noise floor]; on Calmar+cost+symmetric-tails, UCO wins. Same edge -> vehicle = a RISK choice (USO safer/higher-DSR vs UCO higher-return/cost-robust). ALL GATES PASSED except Holm-Bonferroni. PROPOSE oil-reversion (USO or UCO) as 3rd-mechanism book seat -> user/Opus.

FINDING (β200 router, scripts/beta_router.py): β200 = frac TRAIN bars w/ positive 200-bar fwd return, emitted via leak-safe footer injection. GLD 0.532 (≈Wang's 0.53 EXACT — validates our bars/fwd-returns match his methodology), QQQ 0.797 (drift), USO 0.532, UCO 0.503. KEY: β200 does NOT route trend-vs-reversion — oil (reversion) sits at β≈0.5 just like GLD (trend); both SYMMETRIC. β200 separates SYMMETRIC (≈0.5 -> exploitable structure, run ML) vs DRIFT (≫0.6 -> buy-hold wins, skip) = a BUY-HOLD PRE-FILTER, matching Wang's Q&A ('βn≫0.5 -> always-1 wins -> nothing to discriminate'). Trend-vs-reversion needs a 2nd signal = bar-return AUTOCORRELATION (ρ: + -> trend persistent like GLD; − -> mean-reverting like oil). Refines [[wang-investigation]] β200 claim.

FINDING (autocorr router = informative NULL, scripts/mechanism_router.py): bar-return autocorr rho1/2/3 are ALL noise-level (|rho|<=0.03; GLD rho1 +0.025, USO -0.010, UCO +0.020) — does NOT discriminate trend vs reversion. WHY: rho~0 everywhere = logdollar bars are near-IID, which is Wang's AXIS-DESIGN GOAL achieved -> bars too clean to carry mechanism at lag-1; trend-vs-reversion lives in the FORWARD-RETURN/LABEL structure, not bar autocorr. Also IWM beta200=0.75 (drift, yet marginal 0.665 edge -> beta200 is a PRIOR not absolute). ROUTER CONCLUSION: beta200 = buy-hold filter (WORKS, skip beta>>0.6); trend-vs-reversion needs a small {trend_leg,revert} labeler tournament on the symmetric survivors (no cheap statistic routes it).

top_insights.title: Meta-labeling crosses Calmar>3
top_insights.body: A secondary 'is-the-primary-right' decision model (Wang's first-public technique) lifts EEM 2.43->4.03 — the first edge from a new method, trials-significant, clearing Wang's flagship Calmar>3 bar.

top_insights.title: Causal mean-reversion features unlock mean-reverting ETFs
top_insights.detail: Adding causal RSI + Bollinger-%b oscillators (③) took TLT from 0.31 → 1.52 Calmar. Control (same axis/labeler, no features) stays at −0.14, so the lift is the features, not leakage.

top_insights.title: Ensembling two comparable labelers lifts; one dominant signal dilutes
top_insights.detail: Averaging calibrated triple_barrier + BGM predictions lifted IWM 0.93 → 1.14, but dilutes TLT (one signal already dominates). Ensemble only when two legs are individually strong.

top_insights.title: No-lookahead invariant: never filter trading bars by a future label
top_insights.detail: Two enforced rules: (1) test/val predictions cover EVERY causal bar — a future label may filter only the FIT set, never which OOS bars trade; (2) bar thresholds fit on TRAIN minutes, not the full series (a magnitude-axis full-series threshold leaked OOS into bar placement — found + fixed).

top_insights.title: Selectivity beats exposure when directional accuracy is thin
top_insights.detail: On IWM's ensemble a tighter entry threshold (0.45) beat a looser one (0.40, no vol-throttle): +1.14 vs +0.94. Trade less, trade better.

top_insights.title: triple_barrier + ③-features recipe ports across mean-reverting ETFs
top_insights.detail: The same labeler+features stack that unlocked TLT also lifted IWM — a transferable recipe, not a per-ETF fluke. Try it first on any mean-reverter.
